section_text_parts: You could use the same technique to extend the JSON Schema and add your own custom extra info.
For example you could use it to add metadata for a frontend user interface, etc.
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text:
You could use the same technique to extend the JSON Schema and add your own custom extra info.

For example you could use it to add metadata for a frontend user interface, etc.

section_text_parts: OpenAPI 3.1.0 (used since FastAPI 0.99.0) added support for examples , which is part of the JSON Schema standard.

Before that, it only supported the keyword example with a single example. That is still supported by OpenAPI 3.1.0, but is deprecated and is not part of the JSON Schema standard. So you are encouraged to migrate example to examples . 🤓
You can read more at the end of this page.
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text:
OpenAPI 3.1.0 (used since FastAPI 0.99.0) added support for examples , which is part of the JSON Schema standard.

Before that, it only supported the keyword example with a single example. That is still supported by OpenAPI 3.1.0, but is deprecated and is not part of the JSON Schema standard. So you are encouraged to migrate example to examples . 🤓

You can read more at the end of this page.

section_text_parts: If you are already using FastAPI version 0.99.0 or above , you can probably skip these details.
They are more relevant for older versions, before OpenAPI 3.1.0 was available.
You can consider this a brief OpenAPI and JSON Schema history lesson . 🤓
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text:
If you are already using FastAPI version 0.99.0 or above , you can probably skip these details.

They are more relevant for older versions, before OpenAPI 3.1.0 was available.

You can consider this a brief OpenAPI and JSON Schema history lesson . 🤓

section_text_parts: These are very technical details about the standards JSON Schema and OpenAPI .
If the ideas above already work for you, that might be enough, and you probably don't need these details, feel free to skip them.
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text:
These are very technical details about the standards JSON Schema and OpenAPI .

If the ideas above already work for you, that might be enough, and you probably don't need these details, feel free to skip them.

section_text_parts: This old OpenAPI-specific examples parameter is now openapi_examples since FastAPI 0.103.0 .
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text: This old OpenAPI-specific examples parameter is now openapi_examples since FastAPI 0.103.0 .

section_text_parts: Even after OpenAPI 3.1.0 was released with this new simpler integration with JSON Schema, for a while, Swagger UI, the tool that provides the automatic docs, didn't support OpenAPI 3.1.0 (it does since version 5.0.0 🎉).
Because of that, versions of FastAPI previous to 0.99.0 still used versions of OpenAPI lower than 3.1.0.
tutorial/schema-extra-example::section-0.section_text:
Even after OpenAPI 3.1.0 was released with this new simpler integration with JSON Schema, for a while, Swagger UI, the tool that provides the automatic docs, didn't support OpenAPI 3.1.0 (it does since version 5.0.0 🎉).

Because of that, versions of FastAPI previous to 0.99.0 still used versions of OpenAPI lower than 3.1.0.
